cover_letter:
Hi Scowtt team,

I’m applying for the Full-Stack Software Engineer role. I’ve been building full-stack apps with Node.js, TypeScript, and React/Next.js, and I like shipping quickly without getting sloppy on reliability or security. In my recent work at [Company/Project], I built [feature/product] end-to-end: REST APIs, auth, and a React UI, and owned the debugging/perf work once real users hit it. I’m comfortable integrating external APIs and workflows (OAuth, webhooks, rate limits, retries), and I enjoy the “messy middle” of connecting systems like CRMs and comms tools.

Scowtt’s focus on real-time conversion modeling and autonomous sales is exactly the kind of product where tight integrations matter. I’d be excited to help connect AI agents to tools like Salesforce/HubSpot/Twilio, and to build scalable backend pieces on AWS (Lambda/SQS/DynamoDB/Postgres) as your customer onboarding ramps.

If helpful, I can walk through a recent project where I improved [metric: latency, onboarding time, reliability] and the tradeoffs I made.

Thanks for your time,
[Your Name]
email_subject: Application: Full-Stack Software Engineer at Scowtt
email_body:
Hi Scowtt team,

I’m reaching out to apply for the Full-Stack Software Engineer role. I’ve attached my resume, and here are a couple links as well: [Portfolio/GitHub] and [LinkedIn]. I’m excited about Scowtt’s AI/ML-driven approach to converting leads and the engineering challenges around clean onboarding + deep integrations. If there’s interest, I’d love to share a quick walkthrough of a recent Node/TypeScript + Next.js project where I handled APIs, auth, and performance work.

Best,
[Your Name]
[Phone]
[Email]
